plan: Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure and reduce risk.
Trim SPY to 20% to comply with the single name concentration limit.
Increase QQQI to 25% to favor income and stability.
Trim QQQ to 15% and CRWD to 10% to further reduce concentration.
This plan aims for capital preservation and a large cash buffer.
Maintain a significant cash position for future opportunities.
conservative.read: This conservative approach targets around 1.5% monthly, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, leading to lower expected drawdowns. However, it also limits potential upside.

plan: Sell SOXL and SPAL to reduce leveraged ETF exposure and overall risk.
Trim SPY to 25% to meet the single name concentration limit.
Reduce QQQ to 15% and SMH to 10% to rebalance sector exposure.
Buy ZS at 8% of the book, a high-scoring setup from the scanner.
This plan balances broad index, quality tech, and a small cash buffer.
The goal is risk-first growth while adhering to all mandates.
balanced.read: This balanced approach targets around 2.5% monthly, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces leverage and concentration while adding a quality tech name. Expected drawdowns are moderate, with a reasonable upside potential.

moves.note: Reduce broad index for targeted growth
Add leveraged ETF with strict stop

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Reduce QQQI and QQQ to free up capital for higher growth opportunities.
Trim SMH due to its downtrend, reallocating to stronger setups.
Buy NVDA at 15% and ZS at 12%, both high-scoring scanner setups.
Add SOXL back at 8% to leverage semiconductor upside, with a strict stop.
This plan embraces leverage and concentration for aggressive growth.
aggressive.read: This aggressive approach targets around 4% monthly, aiming for stretch growth. It significantly increases exposure to high-scoring setups and leverages ETFs, which amplifies both upside and potential drawdowns. Leveraged ETFs decay daily, and strict stop discipline is critical to manage the higher risk profile.

plan: Sell all SOXL, SPAL, CRWD, and SMH to eliminate leveraged and high-beta exposure.
Trim SPY to 20% and QQQ to 10% to reduce growth and concentration.
Increase QQQI to 35% to prioritize steady monthly distributions.
This plan focuses on parking cash for yield with low drawdown.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability and future income opportunities.
The goal is reliable income over aggressive growth.
income.read: This income approach targets around 1% monthly, prioritizing steady distributions and low drawdown. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta holdings, focusing on income-generating ETFs and broad index exposure. Distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs, so it's not a savings account.
